title: Coryphantha pectinata
intro: Coryphantha pectinata, also known as the pincushion cactus, is a species of cactus in the genus Coryphantha, characterized by its globular stems covered with fine, hair-like spines. The pincushion cactus has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Coryphantha pectinata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Coryphantha pectinata is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Coryphantha pectinata is classified within the family Cactaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as cacti. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and growth habits, which are typical of cacti. The genus Coryphantha is a group of small, globular cacti found in the Americas, and Coryphantha pectinata is one of the species within this genus. The family Cactaceae is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, each containing different types of cacti. Coryphantha pectinata is specifically classified as a member of the subfamily Cactoideae.

side_effects: Coryphantha pectinata is a type of cactus, and while it is not typically known to cause severe side effects when handled properly, it can cause minor skin irritation in some individuals due to its spines.
